skill.name: frontend-review
skill.summary: Pre-merge code review assistant for frontend projects.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 1 file (12 lines). The static analyzer flagged a potential weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3, which is a false positive — line 3 contains only a YAML frontmatter description field with no cryptographic code. The skill consists solely of a brief workflow definition with no scripts, network access, filesystem operations, environment variable access, or external commands. No security concerns were found.

content.value_statement: Frontend pull requests often contain bugs, accessibility issues, and style violations that slip past manual review. This skill provides a structured workflow for Claude to systematically review frontend code changes before they are merged.
actual_capabilities: Review frontend code changes for common bugs and anti-patterns
Identify accessibility issues in HTML and component code
Check for responsive design and cross-browser compatibility concerns
Flag performance issues such as unnecessary re-renders or large bundles
Report style and convention violations in the diff
Provide structured feedback organized by severity
limitations: Does not execute the code or run a live preview
Cannot detect visual layout issues without rendered output
Limited to the information available in the code diff or provided files
Does not replace human review for complex architectural decisions

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Use this skill to automatically review a frontend pull request before a human reviewer looks at it, catching obvious issues early.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams using pull request workflows

use_cases.title: Solo Developer Self-Review
use_cases.description: Ask Claude to review your frontend changes before committing, getting a second opinion on quality and conventions.

use_cases.title: Onboarding Codebase Familiarization
use_cases.description: Use the skill to review example code in a new project, learning the codebase conventions and common patterns.
use_cases.target_user: New team members joining a frontend project

prompt_templates.title: Basic Diff Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current git diff and report any issues you find in the frontend code.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend change focusing on accessibility — check for missing alt text, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and color contrast concerns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing UI components for accessibility compliance

prompt_templates.title: Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this frontend diff for performance issues — look for unnecessary re-renders, missing memoization, large dependencies, and inefficient patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Full Pre-Merge Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive pre-merge review of these changes. Check for bugs, accessibility, performance, style consistency, and test coverage. Report findings organized by severity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Final review before merging to main

output_examples.input: Review my recent changes to the navigation component.
output: Severity: High — The mobile menu lacks keyboard focus trapping, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
Severity: Medium — The component imports lodash but only uses one function; consider tree-shaking or replacing with a native method.
Severity: Low — Inconsistent naming: the file uses camelCase props but the rest of the project uses kebab-case in templates.

output_examples.input: Check this React form for issues before merge.
output: Severity: High — Form submission handler does not prevent default, causing page reload on submit.
Severity: Medium — No error state displayed to the user when validation fails.
Severity: Info — Consider adding a loading state during async submission for better UX.

output_examples.input: Audit this CSS change for responsiveness and browser support.
output: Severity: Medium — Uses CSS subgrid which is not supported in Safari below version 16.
Severity: Low — The fixed pixel width on the sidebar will overflow on screens below 320px.
Suggestion — Add a media query fallback or use container queries for better adaptability.

best_practices: Provide the full diff or changed files so the review covers all modifications
Specify the area of focus (accessibility, performance, style) for targeted feedback
Review findings by severity before deciding which to address before merge
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a substitute for human architectural review on complex changes
Do not skip running your test suite after addressing the reported issues
Do not ignore low-severity findings — they often accumulate into larger problems

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It reviews frontend code changes before they are merged, reporting issues organized by severity.

faq.question: Does this skill execute or modify my code?
faq.answer: No. It only reads your code and provides written feedback. It does not run, build, or modify your project.

faq.question: Which AI tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: It works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it detect visual or layout bugs?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes the source code statically. Visual bugs require a rendered preview or screenshot.

faq.question: How should I present code for review?
faq.answer: Provide the full diff, the changed files, or paste the relevant code snippets directly in your message.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use in a CI pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads code and returns feedback. It performs no network calls, file modifications, or command execution.
